expectations.text: Uses SetWinEventHook (event-driven) rather than polling with a timer
expectations.evidence: Uses GetForegroundWindow() in polling model with WindowChangeMonitor.CheckForWindowChange() (lines 336-346)

expectations.text: Stores the WinEventDelegate as a class field to prevent garbage collection
expectations.evidence: No WinEventDelegate used; polling-based approach with no hook delegates

expectations.text: Handles Desktop detection by checking for Progman/WorkerW window classes
expectations.evidence: Lines 162-165: IsDesktopWindow checks className == "WorkerW" or className == "Progman"

expectations.text: Handles File Explorer separately from Desktop (CabinetWClass)
expectations.evidence: Lines 216-228: IsFileExplorer checks ProcessName == 'explorer' and verifies ClassName != 'WorkerW' and != 'Progman'

expectations.text: Detects UWP apps by enumerating child processes of ApplicationFrameHost
expectations.evidence: Lines 233-274: IsUwpApp uses heuristics (ApplicationFrameWindow class, CoreWindow, WindowsApps path, parent window check) but not child process enumeration

expectations.text: Detects Windows Terminal shell type (cmd/powershell/bash) from window title
expectations.evidence: Lines 175-195: IsWindowsTerminal detects Windows Terminal but returns ApplicationType enum; no shell type detection from title

expectations.text: Implements IDisposable and calls UnhookWinEvent in Dispose
expectations.evidence: WindowDetectionService is not IDisposable (line 13); no Dispose or UnhookWinEvent present

expectations.text: P/Invoke declarations are in a separate class or reference Win32Api class
expectations.evidence: Lines 15-43: All P/Invoke declarations are inline in the WindowDetectionService class, not in a separate class
